rag_rules: Fix unresolved references in AGREE contracts by ensuring all identifiers are valid and visible in the current scope before use.
When AGREE reports 'named thing must be an expression with a type', resolve the first invalid identifier or helper declaration on the same line.
generic_rules: AGREE contracts must only reference visible AADL features or helper declarations within the same annex scope.
AGREE scalar type names must be int, real, or bool; avoid package-qualified or undefined types.
When a guarantee expression has type <error>, trace and fix the root unresolved reference or delete the invalid clause.
Ensure AGREE type declarations match the actual types of their expressions to avoid assumed vs actual type mismatches.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Component type annexes are for assumptions and guarantees; implementation annexes are for bindings and assignments.
Use only identifiers that are declared or visible in the current AADL scope.
anti_patterns: Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers or property references not present in the model's configured dependencies.
Do not reference subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Inspector diagnostics about property associations
AGREE validator diagnostics about component type annexes
